confidence_reasons: RSD-3 zoning code source-verified from Philadelphia Title 14
1926 build date predates 2012 RSD-3 zoning code rewrite by 86 years

nonconformity_details.standard_description: Permitted use (RSD-3 by-right limits to single-family detached)
nonconformity_details.magnitude_description: Multifamily use is not permitted in RSD-3; presumed grandfathered
Existing density exceeds by-right cap by 35 units
Front setback is approximately 50% of the required 8 ft minimum

nonconformity_details.standard_description: Combined side and rear yard setbacks (coarse fallback)
nonconformity_details.magnitude_description: Side and rear setbacks not yet field-verified; flagged as coarse SETBACKS pending survey

grandfathering_status.basis: Building constructed 1926, predating the 2012 RSD-3 zoning code rewrite by 86 years; continuous 36-unit multifamily use presumed
loss_triggers: Substantial improvement (>50% of replacement cost) without phasing
Change of use category (e.g., conversion to commercial)
Abandonment of multifamily use for more than 12 consecutive months
Demolition of a structural portion of the building
grandfathering_status.confirmation_action_required: Pull L&I rental license history and prior zoning permits to confirm continuous multifamily use since 1926
grandfathering_status.risk_if_denied: Property reverts to 1-unit RSD-3 by-right cap, collapsing the 36-unit underwriting thesis and reducing stabilized NOI to single-family rental economics

conformity_assessment.risk_summary: Belmont's 36-unit multifamily use is presumptively grandfathered as a legal nonconforming use under RSD-3, given the 1926 construction date. The use itself, the density, and the front setback are all nonconforming under current code. The preferred stabilized-hold scenario operates within the grandfathered envelope and carries no entitlement risk other than substantial-improvement-threshold discipline during turnover renovations. The alternate redevelopment scenario at 50 units would require a use variance from the Philadelphia Zoning Board of Adjustment.
diligence_actions_required: Confirm continuous multifamily use via L&I rental license records
Verify renovation budget remains under substantial-improvement threshold
Order boundary survey to field-verify all setback dimensions
Obtain zoning verification letter from L&I before LOI

scenarios.business_thesis: Operate the existing 36-unit building as-is, leasing vacancies at market rents and performing only light cosmetic turnover refreshes. Preserves grandfathered legal nonconforming use; no displacement; no entitlement risk.

use_mix.notes: All floors residential; no commercial component

scenarios.operating_strategy: Stabilized hold with light cosmetic turnover only; renovation budget per turn capped at 5% of structure value to stay well below substantial-improvement threshold

zoning_pathway.rationale: Operating within the existing grandfathered legal nonconforming envelope; no discretionary approval required
zoning_pathway.approval_body: Philadelphia Department of Licenses and Inspections
zoning_pathway.fallback_if_denied: Not applicable — no discretionary approval contemplated

key_risks: Grandfathering loss if cumulative renovation crosses substantial-improvement threshold
RSD-3 ordinance changes could erode nonconforming protections

scenarios.business_thesis: Pursue a use variance from the Philadelphia Zoning Board of Adjustment to redevelop the parcel as a 50-unit multifamily building, replacing the 1926 structure. Eliminates grandfathering risk and unlocks 14 incremental units.

use_mix.notes: All floors residential; ground floor amenity (lobby + leasing only)

scenarios.operating_strategy: Ground-up redevelopment to modern multifamily standards; market-rate rents at Wynnefield comp set

zoning_pathway.rationale: RSD-3 prohibits multifamily by-right; a use variance is required to permit the proposed 50-unit configuration
zoning_pathway.fallback_if_denied: Fall back to Scenario 1 (stabilized hold of existing 36-unit building); abandon redevelopment thesis

key_risks: RSD-3 use variances in Wynnefield have <50% historical approval rate
$175k variance soft cost is sunk if denied
Community opposition from neighboring single-family owners is likely

entitlement_risk_flag.risk_summary: Use variances in RSD-3 districts face an elevated hardship test and active community opposition. Historical approval rate in Wynnefield is below 50%. The variance economic case must be robust enough to absorb a denial without breaking the deal.
diligence_required: Pre-application meeting with ZBA staff before LOI
Community engagement with the Wynnefield Residents Association
Comparable use-variance precedent research within 1-mile radius
Hardship narrative prepared by Philadelphia land-use counsel

zoning_extensions.use_flexibility_explanation: RSD-3 is the most restrictive multifamily-blocking residential district in Philadelphia. The existing 36-unit configuration is the only legal multifamily use of the parcel; any other use of meaningful scale requires a use variance with low approval probability.
zoning_extensions.cross_scenario_recommendation: The stabilized-hold scenario is the preferred path. The grandfathered 36-unit configuration produces stable in-place NOI with zero entitlement risk and modest renovation upside under substantial-improvement-threshold discipline. The variance redevelopment scenario is mathematically attractive at the 50-unit count but operationally fragile given RSD-3's elevated hardship test, the 35% success probability, and active community opposition risk in Wynnefield. Variance redevelopment is recommended only if a sponsor with demonstrated RSD-3 use-variance success has the deal under control and is willing to underwrite the 12-month entitlement carry plus a denial-case fallback to scenario 1 economics.
